recs.s: NEW: Smoke Claude failed because the `Install Playwright CLI skills` step downloaded FFmpeg from `playwright-akamai.azureedge.net` / `playwright-verizon.azureedge.net` / `playwright.azureedge.net` — none of which are in GH_AW_ALLOWED_DOMAINS. The allowlist contains `cdn.playwright.dev` but the real binary blob lives behind Azure CDN. Two fixes: (a) add the 3 Playwright Azure CDN hosts (or `*.azureedge.net`) to the Smoke Claude allowed_domains; (b) pre-install or cache FFmpeg in the runner image so the step doesn't need network. Either path takes < 10 lines of YAML.

STUCK FOR 7 DAYS: Changeset Generator is the LAST workflow still pinned to `gpt-5.3-codex` which the CLI auto-routes to `gpt-5-codex-alpha-2025-11-07` (404 at proxy). Other codex workflows (AI Moderator, Smoke Codex, Daily Cache Strategy Analyzer) all migrated to `gpt-5.4` and succeed. One-line frontmatter edit: change `model: gpt-5.3-codex` → `model: gpt-5.4` in `.github/workflows/changeset-generator.md` and re-compile.

PARTIAL FIX HOLDING (15 → 2 occurrences) but not eliminated. The remaining 2 failures both in Contribution Check today suggest a prompt template still emits target=* without item_number under some path. Hardest fix is at MCP boundary: reject add_comment with target="*" AND no item_number at validation time so the agent gets immediate feedback and retries. Cheaper than fixing every prompt that ever generates this pattern.

NEW observability: Smoke Pi blocked 20/22 requests (91%) on `api-proxy:10000` and `api-proxy:10002`. Workflow succeeded — pattern may be intentional (testing block behavior) or stale config. Verify by checking smoke-pi.md frontmatter: do the api-proxy hostnames match the current sandbox config? If yes (test), document the expected block rate so future audits don't flag. If no (drift), update.

24.9% block rate today (was 16.4% yest). Drivers identified: Playwright FFmpeg blocks (causing actual failure) + Smoke Pi internal proxies (cosmetic). Fixing the Playwright issue removes ~3-5% of the spike. Smoke Pi review separates remaining test-intentional blocks from real drift.

WAITING: workflow still in_progress at audit time. Re-check tomorrow whether the $9.90 spike continues or normalizes. If 2 consecutive days at $9+, escalate to critical and investigate prompt/input growth.
IMPROVING: variance tightened from 0-217 → 10-48 turns, 3/3 runs succeeded today. If trend holds tomorrow, close as resolved.
DAY 4 CONFIRMED: pin 1.0.52 as minimum allowed CLI version in CI to prevent regression. 22 copilot runs today across 9 different workflows, all clean.
Continue tracking. Today's spike is explained by 2 specific causes; once addressed, expect return to ~16% baseline.
